Definition: The midpoint of the hairline, used as the superior limit of the forehead for facial height proportions.
The most prominent point of the forehead in the midsagittal plane, above the nasal root.
The deepest point of the soft tissue contour of the nasal root, overlying the frontonasal suture.
The most anterior point of the nasal tip, used for nasal projection and nasofrontal angle assessment.
The midpoint of the columella base at the inferior border of the nasal septum, defining the nasolabial angle.
The junction of the columella and the upper lip, a key reference for facial height division.
The most anterior point of the upper lip vermilion border, used for lip protrusion assessment.
The lowermost point on the vermilion of the upper lip, defining the upper lip height.
The uppermost point on the vermilion of the lower lip, used with Stms for interlabial gap.
The most anterior point of the lower lip vermilion, used for lower lip protrusion.
The deepest midline point of the labiomental fold, defining the mentolabial angle.
The most anterior point of the soft tissue chin, used for profile convexity evaluation.
The most inferior point of the soft tissue chin, defining the lower limit of facial height.
The superior reference point of the natural head position plumb line (true vertical).
The inferior reference point of the natural head position plumb line (true vertical).
